vocab.meaning: trở nên yên tĩnh hơn, bớt ồn ào đi
vocab.example: Late at night, the street noise finally starts to pipe down, allowing me to get some much-needed rest.

vocab.meaning: phát ra âm thanh cực lớn và gây khó chịu
vocab.example: Every Sunday morning, pop music blares out from the local market's speakers, waking up the entire block.

vocab.meaning: lọt qua, len lỏi qua (vách ngăn, cửa sổ)
vocab.example: Even with the windows closed, the muffled sound of heavy traffic tends to filter through into my study.

vocab.meaning: làm giật mình tỉnh giấc/thoát khỏi trạng thái gì
vocab.example: The sudden shriek of a car alarm often startles me out of my deep concentration when I'm reading.

vocab.meaning: lờ đi, không chú ý đến âm thanh xung quanh
vocab.example: Living near a train station, I've eventually learned to tune out the rhythmic clatter of the tracks.
In the narrow alleyways near my house, the sound of footsteps seems to echo back against the brick walls.

vocab.meaning: nhận ra/nghe thấy một âm thanh nhỏ hoặc mờ nhạt
vocab.example: When everything is still, I can pick up on the faint rustling of leaves in the park across the street.

vocab.meaning: nói át đi tiếng ồn khác
vocab.example: The construction site is so loud that we literally have to shout over the drilling to hear each other.

vocab.meaning: giảm dần cường độ rồi tắt hẳn
vocab.example: I usually wait for the commuter rush to die down before I start my morning meditation.

vocab.meaning: phát ra từ (một nguồn cụ thể)
vocab.example: A low, mechanical hum constantly emanates from the industrial refrigerator in the convenience store downstairs.
